mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of recovery-phase observations, or Rank IC drops below zero when applied outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns during payroll growth periods are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.5), or the signal fails to predict direction in 65% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets absorb inflation surprises without negative returns in over 55% of triggered cases, or the threshold shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales expansion confirm robust aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both show positive growth
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate increase during a late-cycle expansion phase compresses equity multiples and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day window as investors price in growth deceleration.
failure_condition: Rate hikes in late-cycle phases produce positive returns in over 55% of cases, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.
